source_jobs.title: Staff Product Engineer, Backend (Go, Java)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DASH0

Join Dash0 and help us define the future of observability. We are OpenTelemetry-native, building a delightful, simple, and AI-centric platform that eliminates vendor lock-in and meaningless toil. Shape a product that developers love—all with transparent pricing and cost-control built in.



Let's Architect a Data Engine at Scale.

Are you ready to define the technical future of a product that developers genuinely love?

At Dash0, we're not just building another observability tool; we're on a mission to bring observability closer to the developers; make it a part of their everyday work. We believe that getting deep insights should be easy and accessible. Our customers praise our simpler solution that empowers developers, and we're scaling fast.

We are looking for a Staff Product Engineer https://leerob.com/product-engineers to lead the architectural vision of our backend, joining one of our groundbreaking teams: the Revenue Engineering team, responsible for architecting our usage-based billing system (handling complex metering) and automating the flow of data between our product, Stripe, cloud marketplaces and internal reporting systems, among other things. You won't just write code; you will own the technical roadmap, drive economic efficiency in our data infrastructure, and mentor a team of brilliant engineers.



The Challenge You'll Tackle:

 - Turn usage into revenue. We charge based on data, which means we need rock-solid metering. You’ll build the systems that count every event, sync it with payment providers, and ensure invoices are accurate down to the cent.

 - Drive reliability and risk management. You will own the reliability of the platform, proactively identifying risks before they become incidents. You’ll foster a security-first culture and ensure our monitoring efforts are world-class.

 - Provide mentorship and improve the process. You will define the standards for code quality, testing, and observability. You act as a force multiplier—mentoring others, reviewing PRDs to ensure technical feasibility, and simplifying complex technical designs to align with product goals.



What Success Looks Like (and How You'll Get There):

 - Within 1 week, you'll have shipped your first piece of production ready code.

 - Within 3 months, you’ll extend our billing system and provide more transparency into usage and help our customers to maintain full control over observability cost. This includes improving cost visibility through usage dashboards and forecasting, and strengthening the notification pipeline to ensure timely alerts reach the right stakeholders.

 - Within 6 months, you’ll establish new architectural patterns in Go in our billing solution, raising the bar for reliability and maintainability. You'll deepen integrations with marketplace and CRM tools, and actively mentor engineers across the organization on billing architecture and third-party integrations.

 - Within 12 months, you'll be a technical authority for our revenue engineering backend, having guided the team through major scaling challenges across billing, metering, and marketplace integrations. You'll shape the product roadmap by bridging technical constraints with customer needs - translating usage patterns and cost control requirements into reliable, scalable billing infrastructure.



We're looking for someone who:

 - (The Big One) Has experience with billing systems or usage-based pricing models (Stripe API experience is a huge plus).

 - Has deep architectural expertise in building and scaling distributed backend systems using Go (mandatory). You understand the trade-offs of different patterns and can explain them clearly. Java knowledge is a nice extra!

 - Expertly leverages AI and agentic coding tools to accelerate development, applying deep technical judgment to verify, refine, and own the final output.

 - Understands that data accuracy is everything when money is involved. You test thoroughly because you know a bug here affec

The Opportunity

Dash0 is growing fast in EMEA and we're looking for an Enterprise Sales Development Representative to help build pipeline for our largest accounts. You'll be the first point of contact for potential enterprise customers — identifying and qualifying new opportunities, engaging with technical buyers, and executing targeted outreach campaigns.

You'll collaborate closely with Enterprise Account Executives and Marketing to target the right accounts. At Dash0, we value outcomes, not just activities — you'll be measured by your ability to create high-quality opportunities that convert. Based in Amsterdam (hybrid).



What You'll Do

 - Qualify and nurture inbound leads from events, campaigns, and product signups.

 - Identify and prospect into enterprise target accounts through multi-channel outbound outreach (calls, emails, LinkedIn, events).

 - Research and map key accounts, identifying decision-makers and their needs at the enterprise level.

 - Collaborate with Enterprise Account Executives to hand off qualified opportunities with clean context.

 - Maintain accurate and up-to-date information in our CRM.

 - Continuously improve outreach sequences and messaging to drive engagement with technical enterprise buyers.

 - Stay current on Dash0's product roadmap and competitive landscape.

 - Participate in feedback loops with Marketing and Product to share customer insights.



What You Bring

 - 1–2 years of experience in sales development, preferably in B2B SaaS, with confidence engaging technical buyers at enterprise accounts.

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills in English.

 - A growth mindset and willingness to learn about technical products — you're curious about how observability helps engineering teams.

 - Confidence in cold outreach, handling objections, and booking qualified meetings.

 - Experience using modern sales tools like HubSpot, Apollo, LinkedIn Sales Navigator, or similar.

 - You own your tasks reliably and thrive in a collaborative, feedback-driven environment.



Nice to Have

 - Direct experience in observability, DevOps, or a related tech space.

 - Familiarity with the OpenTelemetry ecosystem.

 - Passion for the developer ecosystem and open-source community.

 - Experience in a high-growth, venture-backed startup environment.



Why Dash0

This is a unique opportunity to help build a generational company. Dash0 is backed by top-tier investors including Balderton Capital, Accel and Cherry Ventures and led by a founding team with decades of experience in observability. We're in the middle of a massive growth phase after our Series B — and we're just getting started.

If you're looking for a place where a great product meets great people, where momentum is real and your impact is visible from day one — this is it.



What we offer:

 - Competitive salary & meaningful equity participation — you'll own part of what you're building

 - Flexible, remote-first work environment with offices in New York, Amsterdam, and Munich

 - €60/month phone & internet allowance

 - Location-specific benefits

 - Collaborative, fast-moving team culture with a builder mindset

 - Clear path for career growth and development

 - Direct access to founders and leadership

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Engineer - Backend (General / Flex)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT DASH0

Join Dash0 and help us define the future of observability. We are OpenTelemetry-native, building a delightful, simple, and AI-centric platform that eliminates vendor lock-in and meaningless toil. Shape a product that developers love—all with transparent pricing and cost-control built in.



The Opportunity

Dash0 is looking for a Senior Product Engineer to join one of our core backend teams — Intelligent Edge, Revenue Engineering, or Platform. You'll build everything from high-scale data pipelines and edge processing to the usage-based billing systems that power our business.

This is a role where product thinking matters as much as engineering craft. You'll work at the intersection of massive-scale infrastructure and business-critical systems — processing billions of signals, querying terabytes of data, and ensuring accuracy in metering systems where real money is on the line. We need someone who cares about the end-user experience and the economic efficiency of every technical decision.



What You'll Do

 - Build highly scalable and resilient backend services in Go — from core ingestion pipelines and edge processing to usage-based billing infrastructure.

 - Solve data engineering challenges at scale, including efficiently querying terabytes of data in ClickHouse and ensuring data accuracy in complex metering systems.

 - Collaborate with product teams to evaluate the economic efficiency of technical designs and ensure maximum value for customers — you're a Product Engineer, not just a coder.

 - Architect and evolve backend systems to handle billions of observability signals with low latency and high reliability.

 - Define standards for code quality and testing, serving as a mentor to the team and ensuring systems remain maintainable as we scale.

 - Drive best practices for Go services and OpenTelemetry distributions across the engineering organization.



What You Bring

 - Extensive experience building and scaling distributed backend systems, primarily in Go.

 - Familiarity with high-performance data stores like ClickHouse or messaging systems like Kafka.

 - A product mindset — you care about the end-user experience and think about the business impact of your technical decisions.

 - Strong understanding of observability principles, including experience with OpenTelemetry and Prometheus.

 - Experience designing for reliability, scalability, and operational excellence in production environments.

 - A collaborative approach to engineering — you mentor others, share knowledge, and raise the bar for the team.



Nice to Have

 - Experience with Java in addition to Go.

 - Background in billing, metering, or revenue engineering systems.

Dash0's AI product, Agent0, is not a chatbot or a feature add-on. It is the core of where we are heading. As AI writes more code, software reaches production faster and at greater scale, often with less human understanding of what is actually running. To ship at agentic speed, you need to be able to run production at agentic speed. This is what Agent0 does. It scans your telemetry, connects to your codebase, and fixes issues before you even notice them. Then it closes the loop and helps you improve your system.

We are looking for a Director of Engineering for our AI team: a rare combination of visionary and doer. You will own the engineering of Agent0, leading a growing team and working in close partnership with Product, VP Engineering, and the rest of the Dash0 team.

This is a product engineering role. You are responsible for building and shipping Agent0, not enabling AI across the company. You will lead the team that defines, designs, and delivers the product. We would love you to be hands-on: prototyping ideas, evaluating new models, sparring with staff engineers on architecture, and setting the bar for what great AI engineering looks like in production. At the same time, you are the person who keeps the team focused and ships real value to customers, not just chasing the latest shiny thing.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Co-own the Agent0 roadmap with Product, drive execution with discipline and consistency, and ensure what the team builds is grounded in what customers actually need.

 - Stay genuinely current with foundation models, agentic architectures, and the broader AI ecosystem, and translate that knowledge into concrete product decisions and prototypes.

 - Champion AI production excellence internally and represent Dash0's perspective on how AIassisted systems should be built and operated in production.

 - Deep hands-on knowledge of agentic architectures: context management, tool use, eval frameworks, retrieval-augmented generation, and multi-step reasoning pipelines.

 - A rigorous, systematic approach to AI quality: evals, regression checks, human-in-the-loop review, and production monitoring.

 - Strong cross-functional communication skills. You can align engineers, product managers, and executives around a shared direction and act as a true peer to Product.

 - Passion for speed and impact. You thrive in a fast-paced, remote-first startup environment where autonomy is high and value to customers is everything.

 - The discipline to focus: genuine curiosity about where AI is heading, combined with the judgment to pick one direction and finish it.
